entries.definition:
ʾ–n–w/y divisions of the night; daytime; to become due, (of food) to become ready; proper time; utensils; to be deliberate, to be solemn; to await; to be very hot. Of this root, five forms occur eight times in the Qurʾan: يَأْنِي yaʾnī once; آنِي ʾānī once; آنِيَة ʾāniyatun twice; ءَانَآء ʾānāʾ three times and إِنَى ʾinā once.
يَأْنِي yaʾnī [imperf. of v. أَنِيَ ʾaniya, intrans.] to become due; to reach the appointed time (57:16) أَلَمْ يَأْنِ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَن تَخْشَعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ لِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ is it not time for believers that their hearts become humble to the remembrance of God?

آنِي ʾānī [quasi-act. part.] having become due, having reached maximum point or degree (55:44) يَطُوفُونَ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَ حَمِيمٍ ءَانٍ they will go round between it and boiling hot water.

آنِيَة ʾāniyatun I [fem. of آنِي ʾānī] having reached maximum point or degree; (of heat) boiling hot (88:5) تُسْقَىٰ مِنْ عَيْنٍ ءَانِيَةٍ and are forced to drink from a boiling hot spring II [collect. n./pl. of إِنَآء ʾināʾ] utensils, vessels, serving equipment (76:15) وَيُطَافُ عَلَيْهِم بِـَٔانِيَةٍ مِّن فِضَّةٍ they will be served with vessels of silver.

ءَانَآء ʾānāʾ [pl. of n. إِنْي ʾiny or إِنًا ʾinā] hours, or watches [of the night] (20:130) وَمِنْ ءَانَآئِ ٱلَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْ وَأَطْرَافَ ٱلنَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضَىٰ and in the hours of the night glorify [His] praise, and at the ends of the day, so that you may find contentment.

إِنَى ʾinā [v. n.] (act of) reaching appointed time, due coming, becoming due or ready (33:53) يَٰأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَدْخُلُوا۟ بُيُوتَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ إِلَّآ أَن يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلَىٰ طَعَامٍ غَيْرَ نَٰظِرِينَ إِنَىٰهُ believers, do not enter the Prophet’s apartments, unless you are given permission for a meal, without waiting for its due coming.

61

أَو

الآن alʾāna (see أ/و-ي/ن ʾ–w/y–n).

أَو

الآن alʾāna (see أ/و-ي/ن ʾ–w/y–n).
